name: Scene Image Binary
description: Binary yes/no evaluation of scene image prompt quality — does it compose characters and settings into a narrative moment with correct spatial relationships?

questions.question: Are all characters specified in the scene description present in the prompt?
Does the prompt describe what each character is physically doing (pose, gesture, gaze direction)?
Does the prompt describe where characters are relative to each other and the setting (foreground/background, left/right, distance)?
Is the scene anchored in a specific physical location from the story (not a vague/generic environment)?
If reference images exist, does the prompt reference every one using 'image N' notation?

If the scene requires a character to look different from their reference image (different clothing, injuries, disguise, age, wet/dirty/undressed), does the prompt describe those specific differences? If no appearance changes are needed, does the prompt avoid redundantly re-describing what the reference already shows?
Does the prompt specify lighting with source, direction, and quality?
Is it flowing prose (not comma-separated keywords, not bullet points)?
Does the composition depict a recognizable moment from the source scene description?
